samples.xml_description:
Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding operations. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output representing the product of A and B. 8 16

Algorithm:

  1. Initialize product to 0.
  2. Iterate through each bit of the multiplier B.

  3. For each bit of B:

    1. If the bit is set (1), add the multiplicand A to the product at the current shift position.
    2. Logically shift A left by one bit for the next addition.
  4. Increment the shift position after processing each bit of B.
  5. Continue until all bits of B are processed.

Note:

  • The multiplicand A is shifted left for each bit of B
  • The process logically simulates the traditional multiplication algorithm using shifting and adding.
